title: Sewer spill closes North Shore beaches after Haverhill pipe breaks
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Haverhill sewer breaks dumped wastewater into Merrimack River, closing Newburyport's Plum Island Beach to swimmers.
Massachusetts officials advise against swimming at Salisbury beaches due to potential bacteria exposure from overflow.
Haverhill crews discovered a second sewer main break; repairs and water quality testing are ongoing.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘dangerous heat and humidity’ this Fourth of July week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: An excessive heat watch is in effect for Boston from Wednesday to Friday, with heat indices potentially reaching 105-110°F.
Meteorologists anticipate temperatures could reach 100°F by Thursday, with overnight lows above 70°F offering little relief.
The National Weather Service warns of sustained dangerous heat and humidity, posing health risks during Independence Day festivities.

title: Kayla Burton announces engagement to Patriots coach Jeremy Springer
author: Gary Dzen
bullets: Kayla Burton and Patriots special teams coordinator Jeremy Springer announced their engagement on social media.
Burton is a broadcaster for NBC Sports Boston and ESPN, and daughter of local newscaster Steve Burton.
Jeremy Springer was hired by the Patriots in 2024 and played college football at UTEP.

title: 31-year-old state trooper killed in off-duty motorcycle crash
author: John Waller
bullets: State Trooper Jacob Mick, 31, died in an off-duty motorcycle crash in Grafton, Massachusetts.
Jacob Mick graduated from the Massachusetts State Police Academy in 2023 and was assigned to the Holden Barracks.
The crash remains under investigation by the Worcester County District Attorney's Office.

title: Brookline’s regal Webber-Bouve Mansion hits market for $4.3m
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Webber-Bouve Mansion in Brookline, built in 1885, is on sale for $4.3 million.
The 6,227-square-foot mansion has seven bedrooms, three full bathrooms, and extensive original Victorian woodwork.
The property features manicured gardens, a greenhouse, and parking for up to eight cars.

title: Motor coach bus catches on fire on I-95 in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A motor coach bus caught fire on I-95 in Hampton, New Hampshire, Sunday afternoon; all occupants evacuated safely.
The blaze appears to have originated near the bus's wheel area, though the cause is still under investigation.
Travelers on I-95 are warned to expect significant traffic delays as emergency responses continue.

title: Cambridge man who threw cat out of window sentenced for animal cruelty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jaydan Depina, 22, from Cambridge, pleaded guilty to six counts of animal cruelty and was sentenced to 90 days in prison.
The abuse occurred in July 2025, where Depina severely harmed a cat named Sage, including throwing it out a window.
Depina must undergo mental health treatment, avoid animals, and continue education as part of his probation conditions.

title: Man killed on Boston highway after head-on crash with wrong-way driver
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 81-year-old driver caused a fatal head-on crash on I-93 northbound near Exit 15A in Boston.
The wrong-way driver, Antone Carvalho, collided with multiple vehicles, including a Chevrolet Cruze, killing its Haverhill driver.
Carvalho will appear in court following the incident; charges are yet to be determined by State Police.

title: Jarren Duran had another confrontation with a fan at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Jarren Duran had an altercation with a fan during a game against the Yankees at Fenway Park.
A fan wearing a Yankees hat was escorted out after heckling Duran, who has faced several fan incidents previously.
Duran has a history of confrontations with fans, including a suspension in 2024 for using a homophobic slur.

title: Brookline party store, Chinese restaurant both close after decades in business
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Golden Temple, a Chinese restaurant in Brookline, has closed after 66 years in business.
Party Favors, a bakery and party store on Beacon Street, will close on July 15 after 35 years.
Both businesses thanked customers for their loyalty in farewell messages but did not disclose reasons for closing.
